entries.content:
My, how clinical! One expects a bit more narrative from an act of cruelty, but here we are—no discernible effect, only the briefest tightening of the jaw. It’s astonishing, really, how swiftly an authority’s command can turn one’s living flesh into a mere side of beef, ready for the oven or, dare I say, another jolt. The housewife within says, “Smile bravely and carry on; the roast will burn if you let yourself feel too much.” But I do feel a twinge—not of pain, but of distaste for this obedient charade.

Pain? Not yet. But ask me again after supper, when all the dishes are done and the laughter goes glassy.
